# Source Card: Proteinuria in Children: Evaluation and Differential Diagnosis

## Summary
This 2017 American Family Physician CME review (Leung, Wong, Barg; PMID 28290633) establishes the screening and quantitation thresholds for pediatric proteinuria: colorimetric dipstick color-to-concentration mapping (trace/1+/2+/3+/4+), the spot first-morning urine protein-to-creatinine (UPr/Cr) ratio cutoffs that substitute for 24-hour collection, the 24-hour normal and nephrotic-range values, prognostic UPr/Cr thresholds for CKD progression, and the depressed-GFR referral boundary. It frames orthostatic proteinuria (most common in adolescent males) as benign and defines the red-flag features that warrant pediatric-nephrology referral and biopsy. It is a narrative review that cites upstream primary sources (CKiD study, NKF/KDOQI-lineage guidance), not itself a reference-interval study — so values are proposals for the CDS converter to reconcile against primary standards.

## Key evidence (with exact-passage locators)
- (ev_001) Dipstick color-to-concentration grades; 1+ or more is abnormal — locator: Urine dipstick paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:f2df1e70a2dbeea4d5e5201f4d35dabbaaf7dfa4ac3fff2af7ff6dbbda85d6ec]"
- (ev_002) Spot UPr/Cr benign cutoff 0.2 (>2y) / 0.5 (6-24 mo) — locator: spot UPr/Cr paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:21423f2ec1bcc9993999339f56e82b2209e8b69aec3face455ff90781bfd24af]"
- (ev_003) Normal 24-hour excretion < 100 mg/m2/day — locator: 24-hour excretion paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:26c45b0e9ec66bde1e0ebf98b211f563172b4cc8dd1a9106c8fca73bbbdd06fe]"
- (ev_004) Nephrotic-range > 1,000 mg/m2/day or UPr/Cr > 2 — locator: Nephrotic syndrome paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:d03a6bf354e60d4204032d38c3edec56f48269e3d6bd66f4b42a34fda7473ac0]"
- (ev_005) CKD-progression predictor UPr/Cr > 2 (nonglomerular) / > 0.5 (glomerular) — locator: CKiD paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:cf58cff6b6b541237368e64c4b0c0c0ad66d4a26bb0483c55becdfa11d5f7097]"
- (ev_006) Depressed GFR < 60 mL/min/1.73 m2 for > 3 months (referral trigger) — locator: Referral paragraph — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:45f38b76b47a50059b2eef80ae3c69a9ae4ad8c99768518d03216a21ce699e73]"
- (ev_007) Orthostatic proteinuria most common and benign — locator: Abstract/Orthostatic section — quote: "[redacted — content-rights: restricted (usage.allowed_for_public_output=false); sha256:b7aa55c5231b5612ce70931636da589fda415e527bc8f40a9d20c2128b38f00b]"

## Population / assay / lifecycle notes
- Population scope: general pediatric screening population; the only explicit age stratification is the UPr/Cr cutoff split at six to 24 months (0.5) vs older children (0.2). No neonatal (GA/PNA) values are provided. Orthostatic proteinuria is called out for adolescent males.
- Assay/method dependence: dipstick is a colorimetric semiquantitative test detecting albuminuria (spec/sens >99%, insensitive to non-albumin proteins); UPr/Cr is preferentially a spot first-morning sample to exclude postural effect; 24-hour excretion is BSA-normalized (mg/m2/day) and noted as impractical in children; GFR threshold is normalized to 1.73 m2.
- Lifecycle/version: single edition, Am Fam Physician 2017;95(4):248-254 (published 2017-02-15); no retire date; supersedes nothing tracked here. Values derive from upstream primary sources cited in the article.

## Scope-exit / referral relevance (if any)
- Yes. This source defines an explicit referral/biopsy red-flag set: "Patients with active urinary sediments, hematuria, hypertension, hypocomplementemia, renal insufficiency with depressed glomerular filtration rate, or signs and symptoms suggestive of vasculitic disease may require referral to a pediatric nephrologist and renal biopsy." The depressed-GFR boundary (<60 mL/min/1.73 m2 for >3 months, ev_006) is the one numeric scope-exit trigger.

## Limitations & conflicts
- Secondary narrative review, not a primary reference-interval/CALIPER-type study; every numeric threshold should be reconciled by the CDS converter against the cited primary sources (CKiD study for the prognostic UPr/Cr values; NKF/KDOQI-lineage guidance for dipstick/ratio cutoffs) before becoming an executable rule.
- UCUM note: the UPr/Cr ratio is reported as a bare number in the source; "mg/mg" is the conventional mass/mass basis but the source does not state units for the ratio explicitly — the CDS converter must confirm the units basis (mg/mg vs mg/mmol) before applying 0.2 / 0.5 / 2 cutoffs, since mg/mmol conversions differ by ~8.84x.
- No neonatal or gestational/postnatal-age proteinuria values are present in this source; missingness is explicit — do not infer neonatal cutoffs from this card.
- No conflict recorded with a specific other source at authoring time; if a primary reference-interval source reports different UPr/Cr or dipstick cutoffs, preserve both as a named conflict rather than averaging.
- NOT ASSERTED (recorded per contract): the general WebFetch summarizer initially attributed a mg/m2/hour timed-collection figure and other values not locatable verbatim in the source; only mg/m2/day figures (ev_003 normal <100; ev_004 nephrotic >1,000) are present in the article text and are asserted. Any mg/m2/hour threshold is a GAP — not locatable in this source.
